questions.question: Which country has suspended groundnut imports from India due to aflatoxin concerns in September 2025?
questions.explanation: Indonesia suspended import of Indian groundnuts from 3 September 2025 due to Aflatoxin concerns. Suspension threatens new crop arrivals in a month, with groundnut sown on 48 lakh hectares this kharif season. Gujarat expects record 66 lakh tonnes, but prices remain below Minimum Support Price (MSP). Indonesia is India’s top buyer, importing one-third of exports — 2.77 lakh tonnes worth $280 million in 2024–25. Aflatoxins are toxic compounds from fungi Aspergillus flavus and Aspergillus parasiticus, linked to liver cancer and health risks.

questions.question: World Alzheimer’s Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Alzheimer’s Day is observed every year on 21 September as part of Alzheimer’s Month to create awareness about dementia and Alzheimer’s. The day supports families of Alzheimer-affected patients to manage and fight the disease. Alzheimer’s is a severe brain disorder causing memory loss, confusion, and impairing learning, thinking, reasoning, decision-making, and attention, affecting daily life and emotions. September is designated as Alzheimer’s Month, with global societies organising activities to educate on symptoms, early diagnosis, treatment, and provide social and financial support. The 2025 theme is “Ask About Dementia. Ask About Alzheimer’s” to reduce stigma and promote dementia-friendly attitudes.

questions.question: A pod of striped dolphins was recently spotted in which Indian city?
questions.explanation: A pod of striped dolphins, uncommon in Andhra Pradesh waters, was spotted off Visakhapatnam. Striped dolphins are streamlined oceanic dolphins, similar in shape and size to common dolphins, averaging 2.2–2.3 m in length. They are found in temperate and tropical oceans, preferring deeper offshore waters over continental shelves. Typically, they form tight groups of 25–100 individuals. Striped dolphins exhibit roto-tailing, leaping and rotating their tails in the air. Conservation status is Least Concern according to the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) Red List.

questions.question: Papikonda National Park is located in which state?
questions.explanation: A study documented 51 species of herpetofauna in Papikonda National Park, Eastern Ghats, Andhra Pradesh. The list includes 18 amphibians, 21 lizards, 10 snakes, and 2 turtles. Three species—Minervarya kalinga, Sphaerotheca maskeyi, and Hemidactylus kangerensis—were recorded for the first time in Andhra Pradesh. According to IUCN Red List (2024), 46 species are Least Concern, Hemidactylus kangerensis is Endangered, Lissemys punctata is Vulnerable, and three are unassessed. Papikonda National Park is located in East Godavari and West Godavari Districts of Andhra Pradesh.

questions.question: Yuan Wang 5 is a space and missile tracking ship developed by which country?
questions.explanation: China’s spy ship Yuan Wang 5 has returned to the Indian Ocean region near India, three years after docking at Sri Lanka’s Hambantota Port in August 2022. India is preparing for a hypersonic long-range anti-ship missile test over the Bay of Bengal (Sept 24–25, 2025), raising New Delhi’s security concerns over Yuan Wang 5’s presence. It is a satellite and missile tracking vessel of the Chinese People’s Liberation Army (PLA) Navy with dual-use snooping capability. It validates intercontinental ballistic missiles (ICBMs) and hypersonic weapons, collecting telemetry data for performance and readiness. The ship displaces 25,000 tons, is 222 metres long, 25 metres wide, and carries 400 crew members.

questions.question: Which district in Chhattisgarh has recently declared 75 of its village panchayats as “Child Marriage-Free Panchayats”?
questions.explanation: Surajpur district, Chhattisgarh, declared 75 village panchayats as “Child Marriage-Free Panchayats” on 17 September 2025, during National Nutrition Month and the “Healthy Women, Empowered Families” campaign. Recognition was given as no child marriage cases were recorded in two years. Success credited to Women and Child Development Department, Anganwadi workers, panchayat leaders, NGOs, and community participation. Awareness drives promoted child rights, education, and women’s empowerment, leading parents to prioritize education over early marriage.

questions.question: What is the name of the internship programme launched by National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) for September 2025?
questions.explanation: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) of India launched its Online Short Term Internship Programme (OSTI) for September 2025 on 22 September. From 896 applications, 80 students from 21 States and Union Territories were selected for the two-week programme running until 3 October 2025. The programme aims to provide understanding of human rights promotion and protection in India. Focus areas include constitutional rights, civil, political, social, cultural rights, climate change, artificial intelligence, cybercrime, and gig economy exploitation. Interns are trained in international human rights law, domestic issues, and advocacy strategies to become informed human rights ambassadors.

questions.question: Which West African nations announced their withdrawal from the International Criminal Court (ICC) in September 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, Burkina Faso, Mali, and Niger announced withdrawal from the International Criminal Court (ICC), calling it a neo-colonial tool. International Criminal Court (ICC) was created by the Rome Statute in 1998, which entered into force on 1 July 2002. It is the only permanent international criminal tribunal in the world. The Court has 125 member countries, but China, India, Israel, Russia, and the United States are not parties. ICC is funded by State Parties’ contributions and voluntary donations from governments, organizations, and entities.

questions.question: What is the theme for International Day of Sign Languages 2025?

options.label: B
options.text: Sign up for Sign Language Rights

options.label: C
options.text: No Human Rights Without Sign Language Rights

answer.label: C
answer.text: No Human Rights Without Sign Language Rights

questions.explanation: International Day of Sign Languages is celebrated on 23rd September every year. Union Minister for Social Justice and Empowerment, Dr. Virendra Kumar, attended a programme in New Delhi organised by the Indian Sign Language Research and Training Centre. Winners of the 8th National Indian Sign Language Competition were also felicitated. The United Nations General Assembly declared 23rd September as this day to raise awareness of sign language. This year’s theme was “No Human Rights Without Sign Language Rights.”

questions.question: What nickname has China’s meteorological agency given to Super Typhoon Ragasa?
questions.explanation: Super Typhoon Ragasa, the strongest tropical cyclone of 2025, hit southern China. Ragasa, called the “King of Storms” by China’s meteorological agency, may bring waves up to seven metres and severe coastal flooding. Authorities issued urgent warnings, advising residents to stay indoors unless part of emergency response teams. Ragasa previously hit Taiwan, injuring six and cancelling 100+ flights, and the Philippines, causing one death and 10,000+ displacements. Experts warn climate change may intensify such storms in the future.
